title: Relations Between Australia and China as Frozen as the Northern Hemisphere
section_path: Relations Between Australia and China as Frozen as the Northern Hemisphere
section_path_text: Relations Between Australia and China as Frozen as the Northern Hemisphere
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
Image reference: australia china.jpg | assets/2021-01-08_relations-between-australia-and-china-as-frozen-as-the-northern-hemisphere_img_australiachina_ef1989f91ba4.jpg
The Northern hemisphere is currently facing a colder than usual winter, which proves to be good news for energy prices. The cold spell has depleted inventories of oil, LNG and coal in north-eastern Asia, where the mercury has fallen the most compared to the norm. The increase in demand for heating have seen oil refinery output increasing by seven percent, compared to a year ago, in Japan and South-Korea considering tapping into the nation's strategic reserves.
